questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the role of slip rings in an AC generator
questions.question: What is the purpose of the slip rings in an AC generator?
options: They store charge so the output stays direct current.
They make the magnetic field disappear every half turn.
They increase the resistance of the coil to zero.
They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow the coil to keep rotating while the output alternates.
questions.explanation: Slip rings maintain contact while allowing the current to alternate as the coil turns.
questions.senNote: Slip rings maintain contact while allowing the current to alternate as the coil turns.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the field around a current-carrying wire
questions.question: What is the shape of the magnetic field around a straight current-carrying wire?
options: Straight lines pointing away from the wire
A single line only above the wire
A field that is always zero outside the wire
Concentric circles around the wire
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around a straight current-carrying wire forms concentric circles.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around a straight current-carrying wire forms concentric circles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Compare magnetically soft and hard materials
questions.question: Which statement about a magnetically soft material is correct?
options: It is hard to magnetise but easy to demagnetise.
It stays magnetised strongly and is best for permanent magnets.
It cannot be used in an electromagnet.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Magnetically soft iron is useful in electromagnets because it magnetises and demagnetises easily.
questions.senNote: Magnetically soft iron is useful in electromagnets because it magnetises and demagnetises easily.

options: It is hard to magnetise but easy to demagnetise.
It stays magnetised strongly and is best for permanent magnets.
It cannot be used in an electromagnet.
It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.correctAnswer: It is easy to magnetise and easy to demagnetise.
questions.explanation: Magnetically soft iron is useful in electromagnets because it magnetises and demagnetises easily.
questions.senNote: Magnetically soft iron is useful in electromagnets because it magnetises and demagnetises easily.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Predict attraction and repulsion between poles
questions.question: What happens when the north pole of one magnet is brought near the north pole of another magnet?
options: They attract each other strongly.
One magnet loses all its magnetism at once.
They join together and make a neutral magnet.
They repel each other.
questions.explanation: Like poles repel and unlike poles attract.
questions.senNote: Like poles repel and unlike poles attract.

questions.explanation: Like poles repel and unlike poles attract.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use the transformer turns ratio
questions.question: A transformer has 220 turns on the primary coil and 60 turns on the secondary coil. The primary voltage is 140 V. What is the secondary voltage?
questions.explanation: For an ideal transformer, primary voltage divided by secondary voltage equals primary turns divided by secondary turns.
questions.senNote: For an ideal transformer, primary voltage divided by secondary voltage equals primary turns divided by secondary turns.
